title: Testing Efficiency & Modularity Ecosystem Audit
scope: backend/receptor-planner, backend/match-backend, frontend/planner-frontend, frontend/workforce-frontend, frontend/preference-frontend, supabase-receptor (pgTAP + Edge Functions)

findings.description: All three Next.js frontend CI pipelines boot a full Supabase instance inside the unit-tests job. The Vitest workspace correctly separates unit tests (*.unit.test.*) from integration tests, but the CI job runs both tiers in a single npm run test:coverage invocation after starting Supabase. This adds 60-120 s to every PR for tests that have no database dependency.

tasks.description: Split the planner-frontend ci.yml unit-tests job into two jobs: (1) a DB-free 'unit-tests' job that runs `npx vitest run --project unit` with stub env vars and no Supabase step, and (2) an 'integration-tests' job that keeps the existing Supabase-start pattern and runs `npx vitest run --project integration`.
Apply the same CI split to workforce-frontend ci.yml.
Apply the same CI split to preference-frontend ci.yml.

findings.description: All three frontend unit-tests CI jobs run in parallel on every PR, each booting an independent Supabase instance. This triples the runner minutes consumed by Supabase start-up for tests that do not require it. Resolving NEXT-01 (removing Supabase from the unit job) directly resolves this finding.

tasks.description: Verify after the NEXT-01 CI split is applied that the new DB-free unit-tests job in all three frontends contains no Supabase CLI steps, reducing parallel Supabase starts from 3 to 0 for the unit tier.

findings.description: match-backend has no .github/workflows/ directory. Tests are never run automatically on push or pull request. The allocator core logic (CP-SAT solver, Excel ingestion, preferencer) has no automated regression protection.

tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml for match-backend. The unit job runs pytest targeting only allocator/tests/unit/ with stub Supabase env vars and no Docker step.

findings.description: No CI job exists that invokes only the Vitest unit project (npx vitest run --project unit) independently of the integration project. The workspace split exists at the Vitest level but is never exercised in isolation in CI. A standalone DB-free job would provide sub-30-second feedback on logic regressions.

tasks.description: As part of the NEXT-01 split, ensure the new unit-tests job script uses `npx vitest run --project unit` (not `npm run test:coverage`) so only *.unit.test.* files execute. Document the contract in the job step name: 'Run unit tests (DB-free)'.

findings.description: All match-backend tests reside in a flat directory with no unit/, integration/, or e2e/ subdirectories. Logic-only tests (test_solver.py, test_models.py, test_preferences.py) coexist with live-DB tests (test_supabase_integration.py). Running pytest picks up all files indiscriminately.

tasks.description: Restructure allocator/tests/ to match the receptor-planner pattern: create unit/, integration/, and e2e/ subdirectories. Move test_solver.py, test_models.py, test_preferences.py, test_excel_ingestion.py, test_api.py, test_service.py to unit/. Move test_supabase_integration.py to integration/. Update pyproject.toml testpaths accordingly.

findings.description: test_supabase_end_to_end_integration calls get_supabase_client() and supabase_populator.populate() unconditionally on L14-L29. There is no skip guard (pytest.mark.skipif, importorskip, or env-var check) to deactivate the test when a live Supabase instance is unavailable. This blocks all unit-level test runs for contributors without a running Supabase.

tasks.description: Add a session-scoped skip guard: `pytest.mark.skipif(not os.getenv('SUPABASE_URL') or 'test.com' in os.getenv('SUPABASE_URL',''), reason='Live Supabase required')`. This mirrors the describe.skipIf(!hasLiveSupabase) pattern used in planner-frontend/src/test/security/RLS.test.ts.

findings.description: supabase-receptor has 18 pgTAP database test files covering structural, referential, functional, security, performance, and quality concerns but no .github/workflows/ directory. The test suite is run locally only. A schema migration could break validated RLS logic, trigger transitions, or delegation constraints with no automated detection.

tasks.description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml in supabase-receptor. Add a 'database-tests' job that checks out the repo, installs Supabase CLI, runs `supabase start --ignore-health-check`, then `supabase test db`.

findings.description: The only edge function test file (supabase/functions/planner-orchestration/index.test.ts) contains 3 placeholder Deno.test blocks that do not import or invoke the actual function handler. The comment on L14-L16 explicitly notes the handler must be refactored to export a named function before it can be tested. Five other edge functions have zero test files.

tasks.description: Refactor planner-orchestration/index.ts to export a named `handler(req: Request): Promise<Response>` function instead of using an anonymous Deno.serve callback. This enables the existing test file to actually invoke the handler.

Rewrite planner-orchestration/index.test.ts to import and invoke the exported handler with mocked Request objects, covering: CORS preflight -> 200, missing Authorization -> 401, valid payload -> handler proceeds past auth gate.
Add minimal smoke-test files for auth, invalidate-session, and force-refresh-on-role-change edge functions, testing at minimum: missing auth header -> 401, valid structure -> handler doesn't throw.

findings.description: supabase/tests/database/11_security_policies.test.sql is the smallest file in the database test suite at 572 bytes. The bulk of RLS coverage is delegated to functional test files. The security-specific file does not provide an explicit policy-level truth matrix for all tables.

tasks.description: Expand 11_security_policies.test.sql to serve as an explicit RLS truth matrix for all tables. Each table should have at least one row verifying: anon sees 0 rows, authenticated non-member sees 0 rows, authenticated member sees expected rows.

findings.description: receptor-planner/.github/workflows/ci.yml pins Python to 3.9 (L19) but pyproject.toml declares requires-python = '>=3.11'. Mismatched runtimes can mask deprecation warnings or behaviours that only appear in the production-aligned Python version.

tasks.description: Update receptor-planner CI yml python-version to '3.11' (or '3.12') to align with the pyproject.toml requires-python constraint.

clarifications.decision: Unit tests are those with no external service dependency (DB-free, fully mocked). Integration/E2E tests explicitly require a live Supabase instance. The workspace split (*.unit.test.* vs *.test.*) and the Python unit/ vs integration/ folder structure are the canonical boundaries.

E2E tests are expected to use Supabase and are accepted as slower. The goal is to ensure the unit-test tier is DB-free and fast, reducing tail latency for every PR. Supabase is not removed from CI — it is moved to the correct tier.
act is used for local CI testing (via `act push --job unit-tests`) to avoid consuming GitHub Actions minutes. The .actrc file is committed to match-backend and already exists in the three frontends.

implementationPhases.name: Fast Unit Test Isolation
implementationPhases.description: Remove Supabase from the unit-test CI jobs across all frontends and create CI for match-backend.
implementationPhases.rationale: Highest leverage: splits CI unit-test jobs to be DB-free across all three Next.js frontends and creates the missing CI pipeline for match-backend. Directly reduces PR tail latency.

implementationPhases.name: match-backend Test Hygiene
implementationPhases.description: Restructure flat test directory and add skip guards to isolate live-DB tests.
implementationPhases.rationale: Restructures match-backend test directory and adds skip guards so unit-level tests can run without a live Supabase instance. Unblocks contributors and enables the CI pipeline created in Phase 1.

implementationPhases.name: Supabase Receptor CI Gates
implementationPhases.description: Add automated CI for pgTAP database tests and fix edge function test coverage.
implementationPhases.rationale: Adds CI gate for supabase-receptor pgTAP tests and converts placeholder edge function tests to real assertions. Both require a live Supabase so belong in the integration tier of CI.

implementationPhases.name: Coverage Quality
implementationPhases.description: Expand security test coverage and fix Python version hygiene.
implementationPhases.rationale: Coverage and hygiene improvements that are lower priority but improve long-term maintainability. Safe to batch as a single small PR per repo.
